observations.recommendation: 5. We recommend that the Head of the Agency shall instruct the Accountant to prepare the Bank Reconciliation Statement (BRS) on a monthly basis, in accordance with Section 54 of Presidential Decree (P.D.) No. 1445, otherwise known as the Government Auditing Code of the Philippines and COA Circular 92-125-A dated March 4, 1992 and such BRS shall be submitted to the auditor within 15 days from the receipt of the bank statement, as stated in the same circular, for review and verification.
observations.reason: Laxity of Management in implementing the Audit Observation

observations.recommendation: 7. We recommend that the Local Chief Executive to require: The General Services Officer to create an inventory committee to conduct the physical count of its properties, plant equipment which shall be made annually and reported on the Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE), in accordance with Section 156 of COA Circular No. 92-386 dated October 20, 1992. The reconciliation of the record of the GSO and the Municipal Accountant to up-date the property ledger card to reflect the true and correct valuation of the said property and submit the same to the Auditor concerned not later than January 31, of each year, pursuant to Furthermore, Sections 119,120 and 124 of the Manual New Government Accounting System (MNGAS) by Local Government Unit, Volume I.
observations.reason: Laxity of Management in implementing the Audit Observation

observations.recommendation: 9. Taking this to consideration, we recommend the Honorable Mayor to direct the Municipal Accountant to compute and record the depreciation of Property Plant Equipment under the Trust Fund pursuant to Section 59 of the Philippine Public Sector Accounting Standards 17 and compute the depreciation using the straight line method and depreciate it using the table of estimated useful life provided under COA Circular 2017-004.

observations.recommendation: 10. observations.reason: Laxity of Management in implementing the Audit Observation

observations.recommendation: 11. We recommend that the Honorable Mayor strictly oversee the preparation and submission of the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund Investment Plan (LDRRMFIP) by the Local Disaster Risk Reduction Officer based on the LDRRM Plan and Budget approved by the LDRRM Council. Furthermore, direct the Municipal Accountant, in coordination with the MDRRMO to prepare and submit the Report on the Utilization of DRRMF on a regular monthly basis which is on or before the 15th day after the end of each month, in compliance with the above quoted rules.
observations.reason: Laxity of Management in implementing the Audit Observation

observations.recommendation:
We recommend Local Chief Executive to direct the municipal accountant to strictly
enforce the submission of report and supporting documents to the auditor within first
ten (10) days of ensuing month in compliance of Section 7 (7.2.1) of the Rules and
Regulations on Settlement of Account RRSA and LGU NGAS Vol. I.

a) Pursuant to COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020, create an
inventory committee to conduct physical inventory of all PPEs of the
Municipality, be it acquired through purchase or donation, including those
constructed by administration found at station;

b) Instruct the Supply and Property Unit and Accounting Office to strengthen its
system of maintaining its accounting and property records such as PCs,
PPELCs, and RPCPPE to facilitate periodic reconciliation of these records to
ensure reliability of the PPE account balances as presented in the Financial
Statements.

d) Undertake all other procedures prescribed under COA Circular 2020-006 dated
January 31, 2020 to come up with the reconciled and correct balances
of PPE accounts in the financial statements; and

e) After inventory taking, direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare the
updated ledger cards for each item of PPE pursuant to the Section 269 of the
GAM for LGUs.

We recommend that Management require the Inventory Committee to conduct a
complete physical count of all types of inventories and prepare the prescribed RPCI
at least once a year to be submitted to the Accounting Office for reconciliation with
the subsidiary accounts and balances to ensure reliability of inventory accounts.

a. Fast track the creation of an inventory committee of the municipality to conduct
the physical count of its Road Network and prepare a report thereon (see Annex
C);

b. Require the Municipal Accountant to update the local Road Network account
based on the result of the physical count and disclose the said account on the
Notes to Financial Statements (see Annex B);

c. Direct the General Services Officer to maintain a Local Road inventory and
Road Map and prepare a report thereon at the end of every accounting period
(See Annex A) as well as keep a complete Local Road Network Property Card
for all roads and its components (see Annex E).

d. Require the Municipal Engineer to provide the accountant and the General
Services Officer with the complete description and cost segregation of road
components for road projects.

a. The General Services Officer to create an inventory committee to conduct the
physical count of its properties, plant equipment which shall be made
annually and reported on the Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant
and Equipment (RPCPPE), in accordance with Section 156 of COA Circular No.
92-386 dated October 20, 1992.

b. The reconciliation of the record of the GSO and the Municipal Accountant to
up-date the property ledger card to reflect the true and correct valuation of the
said property and submit the same to the Auditor concerned not later than
January 31, of each year, pursuant to Furthermore, Sections 119,120 and 124
of the Manual New Government Accounting System (MNGAS) by Local
Government Unit, Volume I.

In view of the foregoing, we recommend the strict compliance with the provisions of
Sections 12 and 13 of Republic Act No. 9337, Section 2.57.2 (I) of Revenue
Regulations No. 11-2018 dated January 31, 2018 and Section 4.114 of Revenue
Regulations No. 02-98 dated April 17, 1998.

Taking this to consideration, we recommend the Honorable Mayor to direct the
Municipal Accountant to compute and record the depreciation of Property Plant
Equipment. Compute the depreciation using the straight line method and depreciate
it using the table of estimated useful life provided under COA Circular 2017-004.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that the management to submit the approved budget
on the Bayanihan Grant to Cities and Municipalities (BGCM), the Status of
Program/Project/Activities Implementation, as well as to prepare separate
Financial Statements for the BGCM, pursuant to Section 122 of the
Presidential Decree (P.D.) No. 1445, otherwise known as the Government
Auditing Code of the Philippines, Section 4.2 of the Local Budget Circular
(LBC) No. 125 dated April 7, 2020 and paragraph 2, Section 364 of the
Government Accounting Manual (GAM), for Local Government Units
(LGUs), Volume I.

In view of the foregoing, we recommend the strict compliance with
the provisions of Sections 12 and 13 of Republic Act No. 9337, Section 2.57.2
(I) of Revenue Regulations No. 11-2018 dated January 31, 2018 and Section
4.114 of Revenue Regulations No. 02-98 dated April 17, 1998.

Thus, we recommend that the Local Chief Executive to direct the
Municipal Accountant/Treasurer to strictly comply with the provisions of the
above stated circulars to maintain a sound internal control in the
disbursement of government funds pursuant to COA Circular Nos. 92-389
and 78-79, dated November 3, 1992 and April 5, 1978, respectively.

We recommend Local Chief Executive to direct the municipal
accountant to strictly enforce the submission of Report of Checks Issued
(RCI) and Report of Disbursements (RD) to the auditor within the first ten
(10) days of ensuing month, in compliance of Section 7 (7.2.1) of the Rules
and Regulations on Settlement of Account and Sections 43 and 44(k) of the
Manual on the New Government Accounting System (MNGAS), for Local
Government Units (LGUs), Volume I.

We recommend that the Head of Agency shall instruct the Accountant
to prepare the Bank Reconciliation Statement (BRS) on a monthly basis, in
accordance with Section 54 of Presidential Decree (P.D.) No. 1445, otherwise
known as the Government Auditing Code of the Philippines and COA
Circular 92-125-A dated March 4, 1992 and such BRS shall be submitted to
the auditor within 15 days from the receipt of the bank statement, as stated
in the same circular, for review and verification.

In relation to this, we recommend the Honorable Mayor to:
a. Create an inventory committee of the municipality to conduct the physical count
of its Road Network and prepare a report thereon (see Annex C);
b. Require the Municipal Accountant to update the local Road Network account
based on the result of the physical count and disclose the said account on the
Notes to Financial Statements (see Annex B);
c. Direct the General Services Officer to maintain a Local Road inventory and Road
Map and prepare a report thereon at the end of every accounting period (See
Annex A) as well as keep a complete Local Road Network Property Card for all
roads and its components (see Annex E); and
d. Require the Municipal Engineer to provide the accountant and the General
Services Officer with the complete description and cost segregation of road
components for road projects.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive to require:
a. The General Services Officer (GSO) to create an inventory committee to
conduct the physical count of its properties, plant equipment which shall be
made annually and reported on the Report on the Physical Count of
Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE).
b. The reconciliation of the record of the GSO and the Municipal
Accountant to update the property ledger card to reflect the true and
correct valuation of the said property and submit the same to the Auditor
concerned not later than January 31, of each year.

We recommend that the Head of Agency the following:
1. Strictly enforce the compliance of Sections 2.1, 2.2.1, 2.2.3, 2.2.5, and 3.1
of Commission on Audit (COA) Circular No. 2013-004 dated January 30,
2013.
2. Submit the list of all on-going and to be implemented government
projects/programs/activities during calendar year 2020.

Taking this to consideration, we recommend the Honorable Mayor to
direct the Municipal Accountant to compute and record the depreciation of
Property Plant Equipment under the Trust Fund, pursuant to Section 59 of
the Philippine Public Sector Accounting Standards 17 and compute the
depreciation using the straight line method and depreciate it using the table
of estimated useful life provided under COA Circular 2017-004 dated
December 13, 2017.

In view of the foregoing, we recommend the Local Chief Executive to
direct and supervise the persons responsible to promptly submit the lacking
documents which led to the suspension of the transactions mentioned above,
in compliance with Section 7.1.1 of Commission on Audit (COA) Circular
No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009. Otherwise, Notice of Disallowance
may be issued as prescribed by Section 9.4 of the same circular.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive requires: a. The General Services Officer to create an inventory committee to conduct the physical count of its properties, plant equipment, and shall be made annually and reported on the Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE). b. The reconciliation of the record of the GSO and the Municipal Accountant to up-date the property ledger card to reflect the true and correct valuation of the said property and submit the same to the Auditor concerned not later than January 31, of each year.

observations.recommendation:
Hence, we rigidly reiterate our recommendation from previous years that the
Local Chief Executive require:

a. The General Services Officer to create an inventory committee to conduct
the physical count of its properties, plant equipment which shall be made
annually and reported on the Report on the Physical Count of Property,
Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE).

b. The reconciliation of the record of the GSO and the Municipal
Accountant to update the property ledger card to reflect the true and
correct valuation of the said property and submit the same to the Auditor
concerned not later than January 31, of each year.

We recommend that the Head of Agency instruct the Accountant to
prepare the Bank Reconciliation Statement (BRS) on a monthly basis in
accordance with COA Circular 92-125-A dated March 4, 1992 and such BRS
shall be submitted to the auditor within 15 days from the receipt of the bank
statement, as stated in the same circular, for review and verification.

In relation to this, we recommend the Honorable Mayor to:

a. Abide Section V.1, V.2, V.3, V.8, and V.9 of Commission on Audit
(COA) Circular 2015-008 dated November 23, 2015.

b. Create an inventory committee of the municipality to conduct the
physical count of its Road Network and prepare a report thereon;

c. Require the Municipal Accountant to update the local Road Network
account based on the result of the physical count and disclose the said
account on the Notes to Financial Statements;

d. Direct the General Services Officer to maintain a Local Road inventory
and Road Map and prepare a report thereon at the end of every
accounting period as well as keep a complete Local Road Network
Property Card for all roads and its components.

e. Require the Municipal Engineer to provide the accountant and the
General Services Officer with the complete description and cost
segregation of road components for road projects.

We recommend that Head of Agency strictly adhere to the compliance of
Sections 3.1.1 and 3.2.1 of the Commission on Audit (COA) Circular No. 2009-001
dated February 12, 2009. Likewise, we demand the submission of the justification
why the agency failed to comply with the above rules and regulations.

We recommend that the Head of Agency the following:

1. Strictly enforce the compliance of Sections 2.1, 2.2.1, 2.2.3, 2.2.5, and 3.1 of
Commission on Audit (COA) Circular No. 2013-004 dated January 30, 2013.

2. Submit the list of all on-going and to be implemented government
projects/programs/activities during calendar year 2019.

Taking this to consideration, we recommend the Honorable Mayor to direct
the Municipal Accountant to compute and record the depreciation of Property Plant
Equipment under the Trust Fund pursuant to Section 59 of the Philippine Public
Sector Accounting Standards 17 and compute the depreciation using the straight
line method and depreciate it using the table of estimated useful life provided under
COA Circular 2017-004.

Therefore, we recommended that Management to:

a) Strictly comply Section 5.1.3 of Commission on Audit (COA) Circular
No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997 to liquidate cash advance within
30 days from return to permanent official station;

b) Comply with Sections 3.1.1.1 and 3.1.2.2 of Commission on Audit
(COA) Circular No. 96-004 dated April 19, 1996 which prescribes the
rules and regulations on the granting, liquidation and monitoring of
cash advance for local and foreign travels;

c) Stop the practice of granting another cash advances to officers and
employees with unliquidated cash advances, pursuant to Section 4.1.2
of Commission on Audit (COA) Circular No. 97-002 dated February
10, 1997.

With this, we recommend that the Management to strictly require the
submission of the pertinent documents to support the Fuel, Oil and
Lubricants expense reported in the C.Y 2019 to the Auditor. Otherwise the
payments thereof shall be disallowed pursuant to Sections 100 and 4(6) of the
Presidential Decree (P.D.) No. 1445, otherwise known as the Government
Auditing Code of the Philippines.

Furthermore, we recommend the management to start the practice of
duly filling-up driver’s trip tickets to support the payments of fuel, oil and
lubricants as required in the Manual on Audit for Fuel Consumption of
Government Motor Vehicles prescribed by COA Circular No. 77-61 dated
September 26, 1977.

We recommend to the Local Chief Executive (LCE) to instruct the personnel
concerned to settle their respective Notice of Suspension, pursuant to Section 9.4 of
the Commission on Audit (COA) Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15,
2009. And strictly follow the rules and regulations on the disbursement of funds with
complete documentation to avoid suspension in audit in accordance with Section
4(6) of the Presidential Decree (P.D.) No. 1445, otherwise known as the
Government Auditing Code of the Philippines.

Hence, we recommend that the Honorable Mayor strictly oversee the
preparation and submission of the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management
Fund Investment Plan (LDRRMFIP) by the Local Disaster Risk Reduction Officer
based on the LDRRM Plan and Budget approved by the LDRRM Council in
accordance to Section 12(7) of the Republic Act No. 10121, otherwise known as the
Philippine Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Act (PDRRMA) of 2010.

Furthermore, direct the Municipal Accountant, in coordination with the
MDRRMO to prepare and submit the Report on the Utilization of DRRMF on a
regular monthly basis which is on or before the 15th day after the end of each
month, pursuant to paragraphs 5.1.2 and 5.1.5 of the Commission on Audit (COA)
Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive requires:
b. the General Services Officer to create an inventory committee to conduct the
physical count of its properties, plant equipment, and shall be made annually
and reported on the Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and
Equipment (RPCPPE).
c. The reconciliation of the record of the GSO and the Municipal Accountant to
up-date the property ledger card to reflect the true and correct valuation of the
said property and submit the same to the Auditor concerned not later than
January 31, of each year.

b. update the reconciliation of bank and book balances so that reconciling items
can be accurately
Such BRS shall be regularly submitted to the auditor for review and
verification.

b. The reconciliation of the record of the GSO and the Municipal

b. update the reconciliation of bank and book balances so that reconciling

Such BRS shall be regularly submitted to the auditor for review and

strictly enforce the submission of report and supporting documents to the auditor
within first ten (10) days of ensuing month in compliance of Section 7 (7.2.1) of

We recommend that Head of Agency must enforce the compliance of the
above rules and regulations of COA Circular No. 2013-004, dated January 30, 2013.
The Head of Agency shall inform its SA and ATL within (10) days after the

insure its implementation in accordance with the provisions of

Recommendation

We recommend to the Local Chief Executive (LCE) to instruct the personnel
concerned to settle their Notice of Suspension, pursuant to Section 9.4 of the

and the monthly report of Source and Utilization of DRRMF and strictly observe the
disclosure requirements of Unexpended balance of Quick Response Fund (QRF) and
the MOOE of LDRRMF in the Notes to Financial Statement.

Considering the amount involved, we recommend that the management should
immediately communicate and negotiate with the GSIS Management regarding the
settlement of this obligation and secure for a Memorandum of Agreement, if
applicable, in order to start on the LGU’s payments for the above accounts and
finally settle this long standing obligation.

a.  the General Services Officer to create an inventory committee to conduct the physical count of its properties, plant equipment, and shall be made annually and reported on the Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE).

The reconciliation of the record of the GSO and the Municipal Accountant to up-date the property ledger card to reflect the true and correct valuation of the said property and submit the same to the Auditor concerned not later than January 31, of each year.

We recommend that Head of Agency shall strictly enforce the compliance of Section 7 (7.2.1) of RRSA, Section 43 and 44 of LGU NGAS Vol. I.

We recommend that the Head of Agency shall direct the Accountant and Accountable Officer to submit Disbursement Vouchers for cash advance and strictly comply the provisions of COA Circular No. 97-002 particularly Section 5 for liquidation of cash advance and submission of Liquidation Report with all supporting documents to the auditor for audit.

The personnel concerned to ensure that the 20% component of IRA Utilization shall be submitted to the Auditor for validation/inspection of projects.

2.)	The Management should present this amount in the liability section of its Statement of Financial Position that will serve as basis for the LGU’s Budget Allocation in settling this liability. Furthermore, the Management should religiously abide with the provisions stated under the GSIS Act and other mandatory provisions in order to avoid the recurrence of such offense.

Recommendation
Considering the amount involved, we recommend that the management should immediately communicate and negotiate with the GSIS Management regarding the settlement of this obligation and secure for a Memorandum of Agreement, if applicable, in order to start on the LGU’s payments for the above accounts and finally settle this long standing obligation.
The Management should present this amount in the liability section of its Statement of Financial Position that will serve as basis for the LGU’s Budget Allocation in settling this liability. Furthermore, the Management should religiously abide with the provisions stated under the GSIS Act and other mandatory provisions in order to avoid the recurrence of such offense.

Recommendation
We recommend that Head of Agency shall strictly enforce the compliance of Section 7 (7.2.1) of RRSA, Section 43 and 44 of LGU NGAS Vol. I.

Recommendation
We recommend that the Head of Agency shall direct the Accountable Officer and Accountant to strictly comply the provisions of COA Circular No. 97-002 particularly Section 5 for liquidation of cash advance and submission of Liquidation Report with all supporting documents to the auditor for audit.

Recommendation
Direct the management to create an inventory committee to conduct the physical count of its properties, plant equipment.  Also, require the General Services and the Accounting Division to up-date the property ledger card to reflect the true and correct valuation of the said property.

Recommendation
We recommend that the Head of Agency shall instruct the Accountant to prepare the Bank Reconciliation Statement (BRS) on a monthly basis in accordance with COA Circular 92-125-A dated March 4, 1992.  Such BRS shall be regularly submitted to the auditor for review and verification.

We recommend the Treasurer and Accountant to prepare and submit  the monthly reports, quarterly reports and final reports/statements and other required reports within the time frame prescribed under the existing policies and to see to it that these reports are timely submitted to the Office of the Auditor.
